LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT - THE COURT ORDERS AS FOLLOWS: AS TO THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT, THE COURT EMPOWERS THE RECEIVER TO RETAIN A REAL ESTATE BROKER TO PUT THE PROPERTY ASSET UP FOR SALE. ONCE AN OFFER IS MADE THAT THE RECEIVER BELIEVES SHOULD BE ACCEPTED, THE RECEIVER MAY SUBMIT AN EX PARTE APPLICATION TO THE COURT FOR APPROVAL OF THE SALE. PARTIES MAY OBJECT TO THE SALE AT THE EX PARTE HEARING. IF THE PROPERTY ASSET IS SOLD, SUBSEQUENT PROCEEDINGS SHALL ADDRESS THE DISTRIBUTION OF INTERESTS. THE AUCTION CURRENTLY SET FOR THE PROPERTY ASSET IS NOW MOOT, AND THE AUCTION SHALL BE CALLED OFF BY THE PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG'S MOTION TO RESCIND SALE OF HIS OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN LOMBARD FLATS, LLC IS DENIED. CCP SECTION 701.680, BY ITS TERMS, APPLIES TO ALL SALES OF PROPERTY "PURSUANT TO" ARTICLE 6 OF CHAPTER 3 OF THE ENFORCEMENT OF JUDGMENTS ACT. Martin Eng, Lombard Flats, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Rescind Sale Of Defendant Martin Engs Ownership Interest In Lombard Flats, Llc By Braunco And Plaintiff Legal Recovery, Llc
Off Calendar Dec-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Martin Eng, Lombard Flats, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Rescind Sale Of Defendant Martin Engs Ownership Interest In Lombard Flats, Llc By Braunco And Plaintiff Legal Recovery, Llc.

Defendant Martin Eng's motion to rescind sale of his ownership interest in Lombard Flats, LLC is denied. CCP section 701.680, by its terms, applies to all sales of property "pursuant to" article 6 of chapter 3 of the Enforcement of Judgments Act. Article 6 covers all post-judgment sales except those that are specifically exempted from the coverage of article 6, which includes all sales of LLC interests per charging orders. Mr. Eng's argument that his discovery of the sale was delayed and is not time-barred by section 701.680(c)(1) is not supported by any evidence.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC's Motion For Order Approving Auction Notice is granted in part. The proposed notice should not mention any particular percentage interest. The following two sentences must be deleted: "Eng contends that he holds 70% interest in Lombard Flats, LLC as of July 19, 2015. Eng's interest is contested." Ruling: The matter was discussed. There are no objections. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. INTERVENOR TONY FU Application For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention Pursuant To CCP 387 is denied. Effectively Mr. Fu contends that Legal Recovery is the alter ego of persons against whom he is entitled to obtain a judgment, yet he has not yet obtained a judgment. MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC'S Motion For Contempt Sanctions Against Martin Eng, For Order Of Injunction And To Reaffirm Charging Order Dated September 10, 2015 is DENIED. The motion appears to be a renewed motion seeking essentially the same relief as was sought in plaintiff's motion for contempt sanctions that was orally denied at the hearing on August 31, 2016.
